summary:
The agent concludes that Chernobyl survivors have an increased risk of breast cancer primarily due to ionizing radiation’s well-established role as a carcinogen through stochastic processes. Breast tissue being radiosensitve further strengthens this link even without specific large-scale study results on breast cancer outcomes from Chernobyl.

The agent reasons that ionizing radiation significantly raises cancer risks, including breast cancer, via stochastic effects; since breast tissue is radiosensitive and Chernobyl survivors received substantial radiation exposure—especially during childhood—a higher risk of breast cancer is inferred by both biologic plausibility and existing knowledge about radiation-related carcinogenesis. This leads to concluding yes—an increased risk does exist.
